project.name: pi-trading-agent
project.description: A Raspberry Pi trading service that rotates bounded equity and crypto portfolios into historically profitable dip opportunities, augments ranking and learned-return forecasts with local news/LLM context, and executes through Alpaca with explicit allocation, order, restart, and live-trading safeguards.

nodes.name: Equity Portfolio Rotation
nodes.summary: Builds and actively rotates a bounded portfolio of configured, held, and autonomously discovered US equities. Purchases require a current dip plus profitable in-sample and walk-forward history, while current holdings are harvested, stopped, or replaced when stronger candidates justify doing so.

businessRules: A candidate must pass price, liquidity, dip, minimum observation, expected net profit, and out-of-sample profit floors
Ranking posture may reshape candidate ordering but does not waive raw expected-profit eligibility
The strategy manages only configured holdings or discovered symbols whose buy has been confirmed
The configured position ceiling is narrowed by available equity capital, minimum order size, and candidate quality
crossDomainInteractions: Consumes learned edges from Return Learning and Validation
Consumes purchase vetoes and ranking scores from News and LLM Intelligence
Delegates every broker mutation to Execution and Runtime Safety

nodes.name: Crypto Portfolio Rotation
nodes.summary: Runs a separate crypto strategy process while the NYSE is closed and applies the same broad dip, return-validation, ranking, and lifecycle pattern to Alpaca-tradable crypto assets. It confines purchases to a crypto allocation budget and supports fractional quantities and continuous-market holding exits.

businessRules: Crypto trading is skipped while the NYSE is open
Entries must clear crypto-specific dip, sample, expected-profit, and out-of-sample floors
Crypto spending cannot exceed its configured allocation or real-time cash
Take-profit, stop-loss, and maximum holding days can independently trigger an exit
crossDomainInteractions: Uses shared decision math and pooled memory
Uses crypto-scoped news and the bounded LLM purchase assessment
Shares account cash with equities through an explicit allocation reserve

nodes.name: Return Learning and Validation
nodes.summary: Turns settled market observations into auditable estimates of net return, uncertainty, and opportunity quality. It combines direct historical dip returns, walk-forward validation, pooled ridge forecasts, and a separately labelled Asset A to Asset B probability forecast without allowing sparse learned estimates to replace hard historical floors.

businessRules: Memory records all usable daily observations but regression training is scoped to decision-specific signal days
Expected returns are evaluated net of configured or live round-trip costs
Pooled learned forecasts are used only after minimum samples and fit correlation are satisfied
A validated negative learned edge blocks a new entry but cannot create an otherwise ineligible trade
The LLM score is the sole news feature in the two-feature ridge model
The Asset A to Asset B opportunity has separate readiness, probability, dip, and edge thresholds
crossDomainInteractions: Receives observations from both equity and crypto evaluation
Receives the signed bounded news score from News and LLM Intelligence
Returns learned and posture-adjusted edges to portfolio construction

nodes.name: News and LLM Intelligence
nodes.summary: Ingests recent Alpaca and public RSS articles, attributes and prioritizes them deterministically, and asks a local Ollama model for one structured bounded market assessment. The result influences learned forecasts and ranking, while configured severe-score and availability policies can veto new purchases; discovery-only symbols may also receive company-specific red-flag screening.

businessRules: Deterministic headline scoring preprocesses and prioritizes evidence but does not cast a separate ranking or veto vote
The LLM assessment score is signed and bounded from -10 to +10
When enabled, an unavailable assessment may fail closed according to configuration
A score at or below the configured block threshold vetoes new purchases but not risk-reducing exits
Article text is treated as untrusted evidence and model output must parse into the expected structured schema
crossDomainInteractions: Supplies the same bounded purchase score to equity, crypto, and return memory
Can stop entry flows while leaving exit and reconciliation flows operational
Uses symbol reference validation and safe public HTTP ingestion

nodes.name: Execution and Runtime Safety
nodes.summary: Constrains all broker-side actions through live-trading authorization, account-state checks, allocation budgets, minimum order floors, cash buffers, active-order de-duplication, and checked submission choke points. Multi-leg rotations are persisted and reconciled across iterations and restarts so a submitted sell cannot silently lose its replacement-buy intent.

businessRules: Live trading requires an independent environment acknowledgement
Unknown or unavailable broker positions stop purchase decisions
No symbol may be touched twice in one iteration
An active same-side order prevents duplicate submission
Quantity is rounded down and kept within the stated budget after a cash buffer
Rejected orders do not advance ownership or rotation state
crossDomainInteractions: Executes approved equity and crypto actions
Preserves risk-reducing exits even when news blocks entries
Publishes observational signal and trade-count snapshots without placing orders

nodes.name: Evaluate Equity Candidates
nodes.summary: Collect the bounded equity universe and turn market history, quotes, liquidity, spreads, and current dips into net-return signals.

nodes.name: Construct and Rotate Equity Portfolio
nodes.summary: Choose a quality- and capital-supported target count, fill empty slots, replace weak holdings, and top up desired positions in ranked order.

nodes.name: Exit Equity Holdings
nodes.summary: Sell managed holdings when bid-side gains, losses, or holding duration cross configured lifecycle thresholds.

nodes.name: Evaluate Crypto Candidates
nodes.summary: Build and evaluate the crypto universe using crypto-specific dip, spread, return, and out-of-sample thresholds.

nodes.name: Construct Crypto Portfolio
nodes.summary: Rank eligible crypto opportunities and buy, replace, or top up positions within the crypto allocation ceiling.

nodes.name: Exit Crypto Holdings
nodes.summary: Continuously manage crypto holdings with realizable-price take-profit, stop-loss, and calendar-day holding backstops.

nodes.name: Learn Pooled Return Edge
nodes.summary: Backfill historical signal outcomes, settle daily observations, and fit a pooled two-feature forecast of next-session return.

nodes.name: Validate and Rank Profit Edge
nodes.summary: Require profitable net historical and walk-forward behavior, then blend ready learned forecasts and risk posture for ordering.

nodes.name: Assess Opportunistic Asset Rotation
nodes.summary: Evaluate the separately labelled Asset A to Asset B rotation from settled pair observations and submit at most one qualifying swap per day.

nodes.name: Build News Context
nodes.summary: Fetch, normalize, attribute, deduplicate, decay, and prioritize recent market and symbol news for downstream assessment.

nodes.name: Obtain LLM Purchase Assessment
nodes.summary: Budget untrusted article evidence into a local Ollama prompt and parse a single structured, bounded purchase assessment.

nodes.name: Apply News Purchase Controls
nodes.summary: Feed the LLM score into learning and ranking, scale new-capital deployment on adverse non-vetoed assessments, screen discovery-only red flags, and veto new buys under configured severe-risk or availability conditions.

nodes.name: Authorize and Schedule Trading
nodes.summary: Validate configuration and explicit live authority, then dispatch only the asset-class iteration allowed by the relevant market session.

nodes.name: Submit Bounded Orders
nodes.summary: Convert an approved action into a rounded, buffered, duplicate-checked broker order and record only broker-accepted submissions.

nodes.name: Reconcile Pending Rotations
nodes.summary: Persist sell-first rotations and retry or complete their replacement buys across broker latency, later iterations, and process restarts.

nodes.name: Assemble Bounded Symbol Universe
nodes.summary: Merge configured symbols, managed holdings, and a rotating discovery batch without allowing an unbounded market scan.

nodes.name: Fetch Bars, Price, and Quote
nodes.summary: Obtain usable historical bars and a current quote, excluding only discovery-sourced symbols that remain unpriceable.

nodes.name: Enforce Price, Liquidity, and Spread Floors
nodes.summary: Reject candidates below configured price or dollar-volume floors and estimate round-trip cost from the larger of configured cost and live spread.

nodes.name: Calculate Dip and Net History
nodes.summary: Compare the current price with its recent high and calculate subsequent returns for historical dips net of estimated trading cost.

nodes.name: Emit Signal Statistics
nodes.summary: Return qualification, mean net profit, sample count, out-of-sample profit, dispersion, win probability, and memory backfill history.

nodes.name: Filter Hard Eligibility
nodes.summary: Keep only current dips that clear minimum observations, raw expected net profit, walk-forward observations and profit, plus any ready learned-edge loss veto.

nodes.name: Sort by Posture-Adjusted Edge
nodes.summary: Order eligible symbols by posture-adjusted edge and then dip size while retaining the hard raw-profit floor.

nodes.name: Derive Effective Position Count
nodes.summary: Narrow the configured position ceiling using equity capital, minimum order size, and the relative quality and volatility of candidate edges.

nodes.name: Build Empty Slots
nodes.summary: Buy desired symbols into available position slots using equal-slot portfolio budgets.

nodes.name: Replace Weaker Holdings
nodes.summary: Sell a held symbol only when a remaining candidate has sufficient superiority and reserve its replacement buy as a pending rotation.

nodes.name: Top Up Target Positions
nodes.summary: Use remaining spendable equity cash to move desired positions toward their target per-position budget without exceeding action limits.

nodes.name: Read Cost Basis and Realizable Price
nodes.summary: Use broker entry prices and bid-side realizable sale prices for each strategy-managed holding.

nodes.name: Apply Profit and Loss Thresholds
nodes.summary: Mark holdings for sale when their realizable return reaches configured take-profit or stop-loss percentages.

nodes.name: Apply Trading-Session Backstop
nodes.summary: Exit positions that remain open past the configured maximum number of equity trading sessions.

nodes.name: Submit Unvetoed Sells
nodes.summary: Submit each due single-leg exit independently; purchase news vetoes never prevent these risk-reducing sales.

nodes.name: Skip While NYSE Is Open
nodes.summary: Keep the separate crypto service idle during the equity session so the two strategies do not compete for shared account cash concurrently.

nodes.name: Assemble Crypto Universe
nodes.summary: Merge configured crypto assets, confirmed managed assets, holdings, and bounded discovery candidates.

nodes.name: Measure Dip and Spread
nodes.summary: Calculate the current drawdown from the recent high and estimate round-trip cost using Alpaca bid/ask spread when available.

nodes.name: Calculate Net and Walk-Forward Returns
nodes.summary: Measure historical dip outcomes net of trading cost and produce walk-forward returns using only prior observations at each validation point.

nodes.name: Emit Crypto Signal
nodes.summary: Expose qualification, profit, samples, OOS performance, uncertainty, win probability, and backfill observations for later gates.

nodes.name: Apply Crypto Eligibility Floors
nodes.summary: Require a qualifying dip plus configured in-sample and out-of-sample sample and profit thresholds.

nodes.name: Rank Eligible Crypto
nodes.summary: Sort qualifying assets by posture-adjusted edge and dip while preserving raw historical-profit eligibility.

nodes.name: Compute Allocation Budget
nodes.summary: Derive the strategy's spendable share of account value, cap total crypto exposure, and scale only new capital when the non-vetoed LLM score is adverse.

nodes.name: Select Target Count and Rotations
nodes.summary: Choose an effective number of positions, fill empty capacity, and replace materially weaker crypto holdings.

nodes.name: Buy Fractional Quantity
nodes.summary: Round the buffered dollar budget down to eight decimal places and submit a GTC market order without exceeding allocation or cash.

nodes.name: Calculate Realizable Return
nodes.summary: Compare each broker cost basis with a current realizable crypto sale price.

nodes.name: Take Profit
nodes.summary: Mark a crypto holding for exit when realizable appreciation meets the configured take-profit percentage.

nodes.name: Stop Loss
nodes.summary: Mark a crypto holding for exit when realizable loss reaches the configured stop-loss percentage.

nodes.name: Enforce Calendar-Day Backstop
nodes.summary: Exit positions held through the configured maximum calendar-day horizon even when profit and loss thresholds were not reached.

nodes.name: Backfill Historical Signals
nodes.summary: Insert prior qualifying dip observations for newly seen symbols into pooled memory without duplicating settled dates.

nodes.name: Settle Prior Observations
nodes.summary: Attach next-session realized returns to earlier signal observations once their outcome session is available.

nodes.name: Record Daily Symbol Inputs
nodes.summary: Persist dip, signal presence, and the available LLM opinion score for every usable watched or held symbol.

nodes.name: Fit Two-Feature Ridge Model
nodes.summary: Fit pooled next-session return on dip size and LLM opinion with regularization and readiness checks.

nodes.name: Subtract Current Trading Cost
nodes.summary: Convert a ready predicted gross return into learned edge by subtracting the candidate's current round-trip cost.

nodes.name: Calculate Walk-Forward Outcomes
nodes.summary: For each historical signal, admit it only when the preceding training window would itself have met readiness and profitability requirements.

nodes.name: Require Net-Positive History
nodes.summary: Gate current candidates on minimum sample count and mean historical return after round-trip costs.

nodes.name: Require OOS Evidence
nodes.summary: Require sufficient walk-forward outcomes and a configured minimum out-of-sample net profit before entry.

nodes.name: Blend Ready Learned Edge
nodes.summary: Use learned edge only after pooled memory clears its sample and correlation floors; otherwise retain auditable historical expected profit for ranking context.

nodes.name: Adjust Ordering for Risk Posture
nodes.summary: Use uncertainty, win probability, and bounded LLM opinion to reshape candidate ordering under the configured conservative or risky posture.

nodes.name: Settle Pair Observations
nodes.summary: Update durable Asset A versus Asset B observations with realized next-session outcomes.

nodes.name: Forecast Edge and Probability
nodes.summary: Estimate the rotation's predicted return edge and historical opportunity probability when memory is ready.

nodes.name: Apply Separate Thresholds
nodes.summary: Require source ownership, target absence, dip, forecast readiness, minimum probability, minimum edge, and the one-swap-per-day flag.

nodes.name: Persist Sell-First Swap
nodes.summary: Submit the source sale, reserve both legs, and persist the daily completion flag plus replacement intent.

nodes.name: Fetch Recent Articles
nodes.summary: Collect recent Alpaca and free RSS articles under bounded network time and size limits.

nodes.name: Normalize and Deduplicate
nodes.summary: Normalize article shape and damp repeated phrases or duplicate coverage so one story does not gain votes by repetition.

nodes.name: Attribute Symbol Evidence
nodes.summary: Assign article evidence only to validated tagged symbols and decay older evidence toward the configured floor.

nodes.name: Prioritize Risk-Relevant Context
nodes.summary: Use deterministic scores to prioritize the most relevant market and symbol evidence for the LLM without treating that score as an independent trade vote.

nodes.name: Format Market and Symbol Context
nodes.summary: Present the model with bounded market articles plus explicit watched and held symbol context.

nodes.name: Enforce Token Budget
nodes.summary: Estimate prompt tokens and trim prioritized evidence to the configured local-model context budget.

nodes.name: Call Local Ollama
nodes.summary: Send the assessment request only to the configured local Ollama OpenAI-compatible endpoint with bounded timeout and output length.

nodes.name: Parse Structured Assessment
nodes.summary: Require parseable structured output and clamp the signed score to the supported -10 through +10 range.

nodes.name: Map Score to Risk Level
nodes.summary: Translate the bounded score into the named risk posture included in reports and purchase controls.

nodes.name: Screen Discovery Red Flags
nodes.summary: Ask for severe company-specific red flags only on discovery-sourced candidates, with advisory or exclusion behavior controlled by configuration.

nodes.name: Derive Purchase Veto
nodes.summary: When LLM news is enabled, block new purchases for an unavailable fail-closed assessment or a score at or below the configured severe threshold.

nodes.name: Feed Score to Memory and Exposure
nodes.summary: Use the signed LLM score as the only news feature in memory and progressively reserve new capital for adverse scores above the hard veto threshold.

nodes.name: Preserve Exits and Reconciliation
nodes.summary: Apply the veto only to new opportunity execution; due exits and completion of already-pending rotations continue for risk containment.

nodes.name: Validate Configured Guardrails
nodes.summary: Load and validate thresholds, allocation limits, credentials, asset-class controls, and incompatible configuration combinations before starting.

nodes.name: Require Live Acknowledgement
nodes.summary: Refuse live broker mode unless an independent environment variable explicitly acknowledges live trading risk.

nodes.name: Dispatch Session-Appropriate Iteration
nodes.summary: Run scheduled equity windows during their intended session and allow the separate crypto process only while the NYSE is closed.

nodes.name: Stop on Unavailable Account State
nodes.summary: Abort a decision cycle when broker positions or other required account state cannot be read reliably.

nodes.name: Reserve Cross-Strategy Capital
nodes.summary: Keep the crypto allocation untouchable by equity buys and cap crypto buys to their own share of account value.

nodes.name: Reject Duplicate Active Orders
nodes.summary: Fetch active orders once per iteration and refuse another same-side order for a symbol already working at the broker.

nodes.name: Buffer, Round, and Floor Quantity
nodes.summary: Limit spend to budget and cash, retain a cash buffer, enforce the minimum dollar order, and round quantity down to the asset precision.

nodes.name: Accept Only Checked Submission
nodes.summary: Advance business state and observational trade counts only after the broker accepts the order through the common checked-submission choke point.

nodes.name: Persist Replacement Intent
nodes.summary: Record the source, target, budget, and rotation kind before or alongside the sell-first transition so intent survives restart.

nodes.name: Reserve Claimed Symbols
nodes.summary: Claim both legs of every surviving rotation so later phases cannot touch either symbol twice in the same iteration.

nodes.name: Wait for Source Sale
nodes.summary: Delay the target purchase while the source remains held or its sell order remains active at the broker.

nodes.name: Retry and Clear Target Buy
nodes.summary: Retry the budgeted target purchase after the sale settles, retaining pending state on rejection and clearing it only when completion is accepted or no longer actionable.

edges.description: Equity observations train pooled forecasts and learned edges return to candidate ranking.
Crypto reuses the same decision math and its own pooled-memory updates for learned ranking.
The signed bounded LLM assessment is the regression model's only news feature.
News score reshapes equity ordering and can veto new purchases at severe downside thresholds.
Crypto consumes symbol news and the bounded purchase assessment under the same entry-only veto semantics.
Approved equity builds, replacements, top-ups, and exits pass through bounded checked order submission.
Crypto actions are confined to their allocation and reconciled through the same safety pattern in a separate process.
